# Acme Health: Stripe for dental claims

Subtitle: closing the 90-day reimbursement gap for mid-market practices.

<!-- Open with the wedge in 3 seconds. The title is the bet. -->

---

## The problem: 90-day reimbursement cycles

- Mid-market dental practices wait 60 to 120 days to get paid
- Cash-flow gap forces 23% of practices to take short-term debt
- Existing dental software ignores claims entirely

<!-- Open on the customer pain. Quote the survey. -->

---

## What we built: claims pipeline as a service

- One-click submission to all 14 major payers
- Auto-resubmit on denial codes; recovers 41% of soft denials
- API-first; integrates with Dentrix, Open Dental, Curve

<!-- Demo here if Wi-Fi cooperates. -->

---

## Traction: 38 design-partner LOIs in 90 days

- 38 LOIs from practices ranging from 4 to 22 chairs
- 6 paying pilots at $1,200 ACV; payback in month 2
- Net retention 134% across pilot cohort

<!-- The traction slide is what gets you the second meeting. -->

---

## From intake to paid claim in 11 days

- Old: 67 days median, 23% denial rate
- New: 11 days median, 7% denial rate
- 6x cycle improvement, 3x denial reduction

<!-- This is the slide investors quote later. Land it. -->

---

## Market: $600M SOM in mid-market dental

- 50,000 mid-market US dental practices
- $40K average claims volume per practice per year
- 30% target penetration -> $600M SOM
- Bottom-up; segment whitelisted; not a top-down TAM

<!-- Bottom-up only. Don't even mention the global wellness TAM. -->

---

## Why us, why now

- Founder/CEO: ex-Stripe billing PM, 8 years revenue infra
- Co-founder/CTO: ex-Athena, shipped EHR integrations to 800 practices
- Now: Ada updates make claim submission API-accessible for the first time

<!-- Two sentences per founder. Skip schools. -->

---

## Competitive moat: integrations and data

- 4 payer integrations live; 10 more in 90 days
- Proprietary denial-reason taxonomy (1.2M denials labeled)
- Switching cost grows with claim history depth

<!-- The moat slide. Don't promise; show. -->

---

## 18-month plan

- Q3 2026: 100 paying practices; $2.5M ARR
- Q1 2027: payer-side integrations live; payback < 1 month
- Q3 2027: Series A on $8M ARR run rate

<!-- Specific milestones with dates. -->

---

## The Ask: $4M seed; close June 1

- $4M seed at $20M post; $1M from existing angels
- Use of funds: 60% eng, 25% sales, 15% reserve
- Hiring 4 engineers + 2 AEs in 90 days

<!-- The ask is the close. State the number, the timing, the use. -->
